secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with fraud/identity domain focus

level_signals: Staff title signals senior IC above Senior PM
End-to-end ownership of IDV and Monitor platform
Partners directly with fraud founders and experienced engineering team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of product management experience in fraud, risk, security, or identity
Strong technical foundation with a degree in Computer Science or a related field preferred
Experience building or integrating fraud detection, risk scoring, or IDV/AML systems
Ability to write clear product requirements and communicate technical concepts to diverse audiences
Comfortable working closely with engineering, data, and go-to-market teams to deliver measurable impact

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: [Nice to Have] Experience with data science or ML-driven systems, including model inputs and outputs
[Nice to Have] Entrepreneurial or founder experience with a bias toward ownership and experimentation

scope.surface: Identity Verification (IDV) and Monitor (AML) — core platform powering Plaid's Protect and Layer fraud products
scope.business_model: B2B fintech infrastructure / API platform serving developers and financial institutions
core_responsibilities: Own the vision, strategy, and roadmap for IDV and Monitor end to end
Grow IDV and Monitor as the core of Protect and Layer by expanding data coverage, improving decision quality, and enhancing reliability, latency, and transparency
Partner closely with engineering, design, and other Fraud teams to move from concept to production with clear launch criteria
Create clarity across teams by articulating decisions, tradeoffs and rationale crisply
Engage with customers and GTM teams to spot fraud and compliance patterns early and translate them into product improvements

green_flags: Clear product surface with well-defined ownership (IDV and Monitor products)
High-impact domain — fraud prevention described as 'one of the biggest opportunities for Plaid'
Partner directly with fraud founders signals high visibility and influence
JD explicitly encourages applicants whose experience doesn't fully match
Plaid network scale (12,000 financial institutions) provides significant reach for product impact
red_flags: No compensation or salary range disclosed
Location type not specified in JD body — hybrid vs. remote vs. onsite is unclear
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
'degree in Computer Science or a related field preferred' is flagged as hard requirement in Qualifications section but uses word 'preferred' — ambiguous severity
